LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT LAUREL SPRIGG, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE COURT ADDS TO RULING THE FOLLOWING: BYRNES TESTIMONY CAN'T INTERPRET LEASE; CAN'T OFFER LEGAL CONCLUSIONS RE: INDEMNITY RE: INDEMNITY SUSTAINED; COMMERCIAL LEASE & DEPOSIT RECEIPT ARE ADMISSIBLE; OBJECTIONS TO CARLSON DECLARATION OVERRULED; OBJECTIONS TO LAUREL SPRIGGS DECLARATION OVERRULED; OBJECTIONS TO JUDICIAL NOTICE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759

Notice Of Motion To Determine Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Be Awarded To Prevailing Party
Off Calendar Jun-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT LAUREL SPRIGG, INC.'S Motion to Determine Attorneys' Fees and Costs to Be Awarded To Prevailing Party. No opposition filed. Appearances: William R. Carlson, Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP (415) 278-9400, appearing for cross-defendant and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to determine attorneys fees and costs granted. Reasonable and necessary attorney fees awarded in the amount of $36,218.66. Costs awarded in the amount of $2,383.71. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT LAUREL SPRIGG, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Robert Burton, Esq. of Kurlander Burton, (415) 392-8920, appearing for Plaintiff; Steven Myers, of Joseph Costella & Assoc. (925) 945-4491, appearing for, Defendant and cross-complainant, Byrne's Properties, LLC, and opposing party; Bradley J. Jameson, of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP, (415) 278-9400, appearing for cross-defendant, Laurel Sprigg, Inc. and moving party; Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment granted. Cross-complainant fails to create a triable issue of fact. The Court adds to ruling the following: Byrnes testimony can't interpret lease; Can't offer legal conclusions re: indemnity re: indemnity sustained; Commercial lease & deposit receipt are admissible; Objections to Carlson Declaration overruled; Objections to Laurel Spriggs Declaration overruled; Objections to Judicial Notice overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
